article_title: Right to Repair Gains Ground on Three Fronts as State Laws Multiply, EPA Backs Farmers, and Congress Stalls on Military Equipment

content_preview.title: Right to Repair Gains Ground on Three Fronts as State Laws Multiply, EPA Backs Farmers, and Congress Stalls on Military Equipment
content_preview.summary: Seven U.S. states now enforce consumer electronics right-to-repair laws, the EPA has clarified that farmers can fix their own diesel equipment, and defense contractors blocked military repair provisions from the 2026 defense bill.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The right-to-repair movement in the United States is advancing on multiple fronts simultaneously, but with sharply uneven results. At the state level, a wave of consumer electronics repair laws has taken effect in 2026, covering more than a quarter of the American population. In agriculture, the Environmental Protection Agency issued guidance in February clarifying that the Clean Air Act cannot be used to prevent farmers from fixing their own diesel equipment. Yet in the defense sec...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent three-front analysis covering state laws, EPA agricultural guidance, and NDAA defense sector setback. Clear comparative structure showing where repair rights advance and where they stall.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 6 sources manually verified. EPA confirmed farmer right-to-repair guidance, Lee Zeldin and Brooke Rollins quotes, Clean Air Act clarification for diesel equipment repair. CSIS confirmed defense repair costs (70% lifecycle costs), $14K vs $3K Black Hawk fin example, $990M rotor blade data rights. Federal News Network confirmed NDAA provisions stripped, Greg Williams POGO quote ('almost completely meaningless'). Engadget confirmed defense contractor lobbying campaign, NDIA 'hamper innovation' argument, Eric Fanning 'cripple' quote, Charlie Schuyler PIRG quote. The Register confirmed same NDAA stripping story. Crowell & Moring confirmed state law details, penalty amounts, Colorado HB24-1121 parts pairing ban.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified: seven states enforcing repair laws (CA, CO, MN, NY, OR, WA, MA), Colorado and Washington effective Jan 1 2026, penalty amounts match ($20K CO, $25K MN, $500 NY, $1K/day OR, $5K/day CA). EPA guidance Feb 2 2026 confirmed. NDAA provisions stripped confirmed. FTC lawsuit against John Deere Jan 2025 with MN/IL confirmed. $33K farmer savings estimate confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Balanced reporting that clearly shows the movement's uneven progress without advocacy.
editor_notes.originality: Strong comparative framework reveals the structural pattern of where repair rights succeed (states, agencies) versus fail (Congress).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Thoroughly verified analysis with excellent comparative structure. Approved.
